title: Tuscan Butter Beans

overview.description: A comforting Tuscan bean stew made with butter beans, garlic, tomatoes and leafy greens. Simple ingredients come together to create a hearty Mediterranean meal that's inexpensive, healthy and full of flavour.

ingredients: Salt and freshly ground black pepper
Extra virgin olive oil, to serve
method: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over a medium heat. Cook the onion, carrot and celery for about 10 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and tomato purée and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the chopped tomatoes, stock, rosemary and thyme, then simmer gently for 10 minutes.
Add the butter beans and cook for another 10 minutes until heated through.
Stir in the kale and cook until just wilted, about 5 minutes.
Lightly mash a small handful of the beans to naturally thicken the sauce while keeping most of the beans whole.
Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
Serve with grated Parmesan if using and finish with a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.

learning.did-you-know: Beans have been a staple of Tuscan cooking for centuries, earning the people of Tuscany the nickname 'mangiafagioli'—'bean eaters'.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How simple herbs, garlic and good olive oil can transform inexpensive ingredients into a deeply satisfying meal, and how mashing a few beans naturally thickens a sauce.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: An inexpensive Pantry Monday meal that showcases how a handful of cupboard staples can become a nourishing family dinner.
chapman-tips: Use cavolo nero when it's available for an even more authentic Tuscan flavour.
Mash a few of the beans rather than adding flour or cream to thicken the sauce.
Serve with plenty of crusty bread to soak up the sauce.
notes.batch-cooking: Freezes very well. Portion into freezer containers and reheat gently from thawed.
notes.leftovers: Excellent reheated the following day and even better served on toasted focaccia.
